extra.reasoning:
The property (monotonicity in syncope) is VIOLATED. syncope appears only in rule r1, which has the highest priority (100) and sets acuity to self_care (the LOWEST acuity), and requires all of {hemoptysis, confusion, dyspnea, focal_weakness, stiff_neck, chest_pain, abdominal_pain, syncope} to be true.

Compare two inputs differing only in syncope, with all of {hemoptysis, confusion, dyspnea, focal_weakness, stiff_neck, chest_pain, abdominal_pain}=true and vision_loss=false:

- syncope=FALSE: r1 does not fire. Firing rules include r2 (chest_pain), r3 (focal_weakness), r5 (confusion), each priority 3 -> emergent; others priority 2 -> urgent. Highest priority is 3, so acuity = emergent (the maximum).

- syncope=TRUE (witness): r1 now fires at priority 100 and wins over all priority-3 rules, setting acuity = self_care (the minimum).

So setting syncope true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, the largest possible decrease. This violates the requirement that setting syncope true never lowers acuity. The witness_assignment is the syncope=true input that yields self_care; the comparison baseline (same input with syncope=false) yields emergent.
